subject: fix(bugs): reversed-marker guard + clipboard textarea cleanup
body:
Two small correctness fixes surfaced by the `bugs branch` trigger:

- scripts/inject-icon-hashes.mjs rewriteSwIconList: added
  `endIdx <= beginIdx` guard. Without it, if someone accidentally
  swaps the `@icon-cache-bust:begin` / `:end` markers in sw.js, both
  `indexOf` calls succeed but the slice logic (`before + newBlock +
  after`) produces silently-garbage output — no error, stale PWA
  icons ship. Verified: reversing the markers now throws "sw.js
  markers are in the wrong order" with specific offsets, aborting
  the build. Byte-identical output on the normal path is preserved
  (verified by post-fix run).

- components/debug/DebugPill.tsx copyToClipboardWithFallback: wrapped
  the textarea append→remove window in try/finally. Previously, if
  any call between appendChild and removeChild threw (most
  realistically `execCommand('copy')` in ancient browsers, or a
  detached document edge case), the outer catch returned false but
  left the textarea in the DOM. On repeated Copy failures under that
  condition the leak accumulates. Finally-block guards the remove
  with `textarea.parentNode === document.body` so appendChild-itself
  failures don't double-throw on the remove.

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed
- Reversed-marker simulation: throws with clear offset-annotated message
- Normal-case re-run: byte-identical output (idempotent)

Tags: bugfix, code-quality, pwa, debug
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: fleet-alignment
Semver: patch
